title: Max Heap Height Invariant Broken Due to Incorrect Heap Property Enforcement During Rebalancing

statement.markdown: In this task, you are given an implementation of a max binary heap. The method 'insert' adds a new element to the max heap. However, the 'rebalance' method does not correctly enforce the max heap property after the insertion of a new element. As a result, the 'height' of the max heap is not correctly maintained. Your task is to identify and fix this bug. The input consists of multiple test cases. In each test case, the first line contains the number of elements to be inserted, denoted by 'n'. The second line contains 'n' space-separated integers representing the elements to be inserted into the max heap.
statement.inputFormat:
The first line contains the number of elements to be inserted, denoted by 'n'.
The second line contains 'n' space-separated integers representing the elements to be inserted into the max heap.
statement.outputFormat: For each test case, output the 'height' of the max heap in a new line.

examples.explanation: The height of the max heap after inserting the element 5 is 1.
The height of the max heap after inserting the elements 10, 20, and 30 is 2.

hints.text: First hint text: Review the rebalance() method to identify the bug.
Second hint text: Check if the heap is correctly built after rebalancing.

editorial.title: Correcting the Max Heap Height Invariant
editorial.intuition: To maintain the max heap property and keep track of the height, we need to correctly rebalance the tree after insertion. This can be achieved by utilizing a bottom-up approach or a recursive method.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force method involves repeatedly shifting elements to maintain the max heap property, which has an O(n^2) time complexity. A more efficient method is required.
editorial.betterSolution: The better solution involves using a bottom-up approach to rebalance the tree, resulting in a time complexity of O(n log n).
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution utilizes the properties of a max binary heap to keep track of the height while maintaining the max heap property. We leverage the heap invariant to determine the height without explicitly calculating it.
commonMistakes: Mistaking the heap invariant for the height of the tree.
Failing to check the height after rebalancing the tree.

functionSignature.cpp: void rebalance(vector<int>& nums, int& height); void insert(vector<int>& nums, int& height);
functionSignature.java: static void rebalance(int[] nums, int height); static void insert(int[] nums, int height);
functionSignature.python: def rebalance(self, nums, height): def insert(self, nums, height):
